name: T3 Vortex Pullback Confluence
edge_hypothesis: T3 (Tillson Triple Exponential Smoothing, factor 0.7) applies triple EMA smoothing with a volume factor — producing the smoothest adaptive moving average in the indicator library. Unlike KAMA which adapts to volatility or EMA which uses fixed decay, T3's triple smoothing eliminates virtually all high-frequency noise. This means when price CROSSES T3, the pullback was deep and meaningful — shallow fakeouts get filtered by the heavy smoothing. Instead of using Vortex for TRANSITION detection (the proven Vortex v3a approach), this strategy uses Vortex as a TREND CONFIRMATION filter: when VTXP > VTXM, the positive vortex is asserting uptrend. Combining T3 pullback touch (cross-based entry) with established Vortex trend (continuous filter) captures pullback-into-confirmed-trend — a complementary angle to the transition-catching approach. The hypothesis: T3's heavy smoothing makes its price-cross events rare but high-conviction, and Vortex trend confirmation ensures we only enter when structural trend agrees.

variants.name: t3_vortex_pullback_v1_8to1
variants.description: Core thesis: price crosses above/below T3 (pullback completion) while Vortex confirms established trend direction. 8:1 R:R. T3's heavy smoothing makes the cross event a high-conviction pullback signal.

components.notes: Tillson T3 — triple-smoothed EMA with volume factor 0.7. Heaviest smoothing in indicator library. Price crossing T3 = deep pullback completed. First system test of T3.
Vortex Indicator Plus — positive directional movement. VTXP > VTXM confirms established uptrend.
Vortex Indicator Minus — negative directional movement. VTXM > VTXP confirms established downtrend.
ATR-based position sizing and R:R framework.

variants.name: t3_vortex_strong_v2_8to1
variants.description: Adds Vortex strength gate: VTXP > 1.0 for long / VTXM > 1.0 for short. Vortex values above 1.0 indicate the directional movement exceeds the average true range — a strong trend assertion. Tests whether requiring Vortex STRENGTH (not just direction) improves entry quality on T3 pullbacks.

components.notes: T3 triple-smoothed MA — pullback entry via price cross.
Vortex Plus with strength gate > 1.0. Directional movement exceeds ATR = strong trend.

variants.name: t3_vortex_wide_v3_10to1
variants.description: Wider 10:1 R:R (15x ATR TP). T3's heavy smoothing should identify sustained pullback entries that hold for longer. If T3 cross truly captures deep mean-reversion into trend, the position should survive to wider TP targets. No Vortex strength gate — tests the simplest T3 + Vortex direction combo at extended TP.

components.notes: T3 triple-smoothed — pullback entry at wide TP target.
Extended 15x ATR TP for trend continuation.

indicator_roles.baseline: T3_10_0.7 — Tillson triple-smoothed EMA. Heaviest smoothing in library. Price crossing T3 captures deep pullback completion.
indicator_roles.confirmation: VTXP_14/VTXM_14 — Vortex Indicator. Used as trend CONFIRMATION (not transition detection). VTXP > VTXM = established uptrend.
indicator_roles.volume_volatility: ATR_14 — position sizing and R:R framework.
